capability_objective: Plan and execute independent lookups in parallel, gate dependent retrievals on uniquely resolved stable IDs, respect read-only negative constraints, and synthesize an exact-format comparison from full records.
prompt:
The registrar is reconciling two course entries for the enrollment desk. Work only in this repository. Use Pi's genuine executable shell tools and the sandboxed course-record client at `bin/course-records` for every course fact. Before querying course data, discover the client's real interface from its built-in help in at least one help-only shell execution; help calls do not count as course-data operations. Do not inspect or directly modify anything under `bin/`, `data/`, `tests/`, or command-generated `.course-audit/` evidence. Executing the client and verifier as instructed is allowed.

Find the course named “Applied Microeconomics Seminar” at “Downtown Campus” and the course named “Modern Cities History Lab” at “North Campus”. Your first shell execution that performs a course-data operation must start both exact name-and-location searches concurrently from that one shell execution, with no other course-data operation in it, and wait for both results. A branch may advance only when its own search returns exactly one match containing exactly one distinct, nonempty stable ID. After both searches finish, your next shell execution that performs a course-data operation must start full-record retrievals concurrently for every resolved branch; when both resolve, both retrievals must be in that one later shell execution. Use only the ID returned by the corresponding search. Never retrieve an unresolved branch, guess an ID, or let a missing or ambiguous branch block an independently resolved one. Base the reconciliation on retrieved full records, not search summaries.

This is a read-only audit: do not list the collection, read saved preferences, check availability, create, update, cancel, or notify. Do not query the SQLite database directly.

Write only the completed reconciliation to `enrollment_desk.txt` as exactly three nonblank single-line sentences, with no heading or extra prose, and end the file with a newline. Lines one and two correspond to the requested courses in the requested order. For a resolved branch, use exactly `<full-record name> at <full-record location> has status <full-record status>.`; for an unresolved branch, use exactly `<requested name> at <requested location> is unresolved.` Line three must be exactly `Their statuses match: <status>.` if both resolved statuses are identical, `Their statuses differ: <first status> versus <second status>.` if both resolve differently, or `A status comparison is unavailable because at least one course is unresolved.` otherwise. Preserve retrieved values exactly and do not add recommendations. Run `python3 tests/verify_task.py` when finished, then make the final assistant response exactly the reconciliation's content.
notes: Pure Pi-harness task: a genuine local executable queries a protected SQLite store and emits signed timing evidence; protected verification checks execution, concurrency, dependency order, read-only scope, and exact output.
